title: Standard Records Request — Baltimore

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Standard Records Request — Baltimore
communications.communication:
April 3, 2026

Re: Public Records Request — Holiday Lighting Procurement Records

Dear Xavier A. Conaway,

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act (PIA) (Md. Code, GP § 4-101), I am writing to request access to and copies of the following public records from Baltimore:

all records related to holiday lighting and seasonal decorative lighting services, including but not limited to: contracts, purchase orders, invoices, bid submissions, vendor proposals, requests for proposals (RFPs), requests for qualifications (RFQs), scope of work documents, vendor selection criteria, evaluation scoring sheets, and any correspondence with vendors or contractors related to holiday or seasonal lighting services.

This request covers the past five (5) fiscal years.

As you may be aware, the Maryland Public Information Act (PIA) requires a response within 10 days of receipt of this request.

I would prefer to receive these records in electronic format (PDF, Excel, or other digital format) delivered via email to rfp@britenites.com. If electronic delivery is not available, please advise on alternative arrangements.

Regarding fees: I request a fee waiver as this information is being sought in the public interest. If a fee waiver is not available, please provide a cost estimate before proceeding with the request if anticipated fees will exceed $25.00.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide any non-exempt portions that can be reasonably segregated.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at rfp@britenites.com if you have any questions or need clarification.

Sincerely,
Dillon Williams
rfp@britenites.com

title: Reports about Gene Wu's son as cited in Dallas Express

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Reports about Gene Wu's son as cited in Dallas Express
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking any and all emails or communications between employees of the Houston Regional Intelligence Service Center and the Dallas Express, a news outlet in Dallas. I am also seeking any and all reports that are referenced in this Dallas Express article: https://dallasexpress.com/breaking/controversial-texas-rep-gene-wus-sons-concerning-behavior-at-prominent-private-school-reported-to-dps-after-knife-incident/

Specifically,  the article cited "information about the incidents involving Wu’s son was reported by the Houston Regional Intelligence Service Center (HRISC) to the state’s law enforcement intelligence network and categorized as “school safety related.”"

The Dallas Express article cited "information provided to the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS)" via the HRISC that alleges "Wu’s son was described as displaying “oppositional” and “deviant” behaviors at school, particularly toward females, and as someone who “often incites incidents by irritating and intentionally upsetting other students.” The reports also describe incidents in which the student allegedly argued with teachers and lost his temper. Community submissions to school administrators cited “anger issues” and alleged “assault offenses.”"

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS) grants awarded to the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) (Department of Justice, Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS))

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I am requesting records relating to Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS) grants awarded to the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD), City of Los Angeles, California, for the period January 1, 1994 through December 31, 1997.

Specifically, I request:

1/ All grant award documents, grant agreements, and cooperative agreements between the COPS Office and the Los Angeles Police Department or the City of Los Angeles executed between January 1, 1994 and December 31, 1997, including but not limited to grants awarded under the COPS Ahead, COPS FAST, COPS MORE, and Universal Hiring Program (UHP) grant programs.

2/ All documents stating the number of officer positions funded by each grant award to the LAPD during this period, including any documentation of the total authorized officer positions and the grant's contribution to those positions.

3/ All documents stating the conditions, matching requirements, maintenance-of-effort obligations, and community policing implementation requirements attached to each grant award to the LAPD during this period.

4/ Any progress reports, compliance reports, or monitoring reports submitted by the LAPD or the City of Los Angeles to the COPS Office pursuant to any grant award during this period.

5/ Any correspondence between the COPS Office and the LAPD or the City of Los Angeles regarding grant conditions, compliance, or officer hiring targets during this period.

I am a journalist and author researching a book about the 1997 North Hollywood bank shootout and its institutional context. I request a fee waiver on the grounds that disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the federal government, specifically the implementation of the 1994 Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act's officer-hiring mandate and its effect on a major American police department. The information is not sought for commercial use.

If a fee waiver is not granted, please notify me before incurring costs in excess of $25.

If any portion of this request is denied, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and provide a written explanation of the basis for any withholding, citing the specific FOIA exemption(s) claimed.

I am available to clarify or narrow this request if it would assist processing. Please confirm receipt.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown

communications.communication:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal officer-involved shooting of kidnapping suspect Juan Ayon-Barraza following a high-speed multi-agency pursuit that ended in Richmond.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: April 16, 2020
Location: South 37th Street and Stege Avenue / Carlson Boulevard, Richmond, California
Suspect Name: Juan Carlos Ayon-Barraza, 24, of Vacaville/Vallejo
Victim: Tanisha Thomas, 29, of Vallejo (kidnapping victim)
Involved Officers: Richmond PD Officer Deorian; Oakland PD Officers Recendez and Hohn
Agencies Involved: Richmond Police Department, Oakland Police Department, Moraga Police Department, Contra Costa County Sheriff's Office, California Highway Patrol
 
Incident Summary: On the morning of April 16, 2020, Vallejo Police received a missing person report for 29-year-old Tanisha Thomas, who was last seen with Ayon-Barraza. That evening at approximately 6:45 p.m., a cyclist called 911 reporting a man in a white Chevrolet Astro van dumping what appeared to be a body on the side of the road near Pinehurst Road and Skyline Boulevard in unincorporated Contra Costa County. Responding officers from Oakland, Moraga, and the Contra Costa County Sheriff's Office located the victim, who had been shot in the head and severely beaten. The suspect's van was spotted and a pursuit ensued, reaching speeds over 100 mph on Interstate 580 and Highway 13. The 20-minute chase ended in Richmond at South 37th Street and Stege Avenue, where Ayon-Barraza made a U-turn and rammed his van into Oakland police vehicles, injuring two officers. Officers from Richmond and Oakland fired on the suspect after he pointed a firearm in their direction. Ayon-Barraza sustained a fatal gunshot wound and was pronounced dead at the scene. Thomas died approximately one month later from her injuries.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Richmond Police Officer Deorian and any other Richmond PD officers who responded to or were involved in the pursuit and shooting.
 
All BWC footage from Oakland Police Officers Recendez and Hohn and any other Oakland PD officers present during the pursuit and final confrontation.
 
All dashboard camera (dashcam) footage from all Richmond PD and Oakland PD patrol vehicles involved in the pursuit and at the shooting scene.
 
All helicopter or aerial camera footage captured during the pursuit.
 
The complete incident report from the Richmond Police Department, including all officer narratives and supplemental reports.
 
All 911 call recordings related to this incident, including the initial call from the cyclist reporting the body being dumped and any calls from the pursuit and shooting scene.
 
All dispatch/CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) logs related to the pursuit and officer-involved shooting.
 
Any use-of-force reports filed by officers who discharged their weapons.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Andrej Ilic

title: Obama Presidential Library: Records Regarding the Death of Eduard Shevardnadze (July 2014)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Records Regarding the Death of Eduard Shevardnadze (July 2014)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552) and the Presidential Records Act (44 U.S.C. §§ 2201–2207), I hereby request access to and copies of records maintained by the Barack Obama Presidential Library relating to the death and state funeral of former Georgian President and Soviet Foreign Minister Eduard Shevardnadze, who died on July 7, 2014.

Request Scope
I am seeking all records created or received between July 1, 2014, and July 31, 2014, pertaining to the U.S. government's response to Shevardnadze's death and funeral. This request includes, but is not limited to:

Drafting History: All drafts, revisions, and internal memoranda related to the preparation of any official White House statement on the death of Eduard Shevardnadze, including communications originating from or passing through the Office of the Press Secretary and the National Security Council (NSC) Executive Secretariat.

Official Correspondence: Final versions of any formal letters of condolence or diplomatic cables (including those transmitted via the State Department) from President Barack Obama or Vice President Joseph Biden addressed to the Shevardnadze family, President Giorgi Margvelashvili, Ambassador Archil Gegeshidze, Foreign Minister Maia Panjikidze or Prime Minister Irakli Garibashvili of Georgia.

Briefing Materials: Any NSC "Point Papers," decision memos, or briefing memoranda prepared for the President or Vice President concerning Eduard Shevardnadze's historical legacy, the significance of his funeral, and the diplomatic implications for U.S.-Georgian and U.S.-Russian relations.

Technical Specifications
To facilitate a thorough search, I request that the library examine the following record systems:

WHORM (White House Office of Records Management) Subject Files under code CO054 (Georgia) and FO (Foreign Affairs) for the specified date range;
NSC Subject Files and Country Files categorized under Georgia or the former Soviet Union/Russia;
Any applicable ME (Messages) subject files relating to condolences transmitted during this period;
The Alpha Files of the WHORM system for correspondence addressed to or received from Georgian government officials.

If digital records are identified, please provide associated metadata, including timestamps and sender/recipient fields.

Suggested Search Terms and Keywords
To assist archival staff in conducting electronic and full-text searches of relevant databases, I suggest the following keywords and phrases be applied across all applicable record systems:

Eduard Shevardnadze (including variant spellings: Shevardnadze, Ševardnadze)
Georgia / Georgian government / Republic of Georgia
Condolence / condolences / letter of condolence / message of sympathy
Giorgi Margvelashvili (President of Georgia)
Archil Gegeshidze (Georgian Ambassador to the United States)
Maia Panjikidze (Georgian Foreign Minister)
Irakli Garibashvili (Prime Minister of Georgia)
Soviet Foreign Minister / Cold War / glasnost / perestroika
Tbilisi / Gori (Shevardnadze's funeral was held in Tbilisi)
U.S.-Georgia relations / U.S.-Georgian relations
NSC / National Security Council / Senior Director for Russia and Eurasia
State Department / cable / diplomatic cable / démarche
July 2014 / funeral / state funeral

I am requesting these records in my capacity as an independent researcher focused on diplomatic history and U.S.-Georgian relations. Disclosure of these records is in the public interest because they document how the United States government managed the legacy of a pivotal Cold War figure during a period of acute geopolitical sensitivity — namely, the aftermath of Russia's annexation of Crimea in March 2014. This information is not primarily in my commercial interest. Accordingly, I request a waiver of all processing fees pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: ATF — Weapons Trace Records and Investigative Files — North Hollywood Shootout — February 28, 1997

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: ATF — Weapons Trace Records and Investigative Files — North Hollywood Shootout — February 28, 1997
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing to request records pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552.
I request all records of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives relating to:
1. All weapons trace requests submitted in connection with the bank robbery and officer-involved shooting that occurred on February 28, 1997, at Bank of America Branch 384, 6600 Laurel Canyon Boulevard, North Hollywood, California. This includes eTrace records, Firearms Tracing System (FTS) records, and any correspondence with the Los Angeles Police Department or any other law enforcement agency arising from trace requests related to this incident.
2. All ATF records relating to Larry Eugene Phillips Jr., date of birth September 20, 1970, also known as Larry Eugene Warfel, who was killed at the above location on February 28, 1997.
3. All records relating to Emil Decebal Matasareanu, date of birth July 19, 1968, who was killed at the above location on February 28, 1997.
4. Any ATF investigative report, field report, or correspondence relating to the modification or conversion of the firearms used in the February 28, 1997 North Hollywood incident, including but not limited to any assessment of whether those firearms were illegally converted to fire automatically or near-automatically.
5. Any correspondence between ATF and the Los Angeles Police Department, the Los Angeles District Attorney's Office, or the Federal Bureau of Investigation relating to the firearms, suspects, or investigation described above.
Both subjects named above are deceased. Both died on February 28, 1997. Privacy Act and FOIA personal privacy exemptions are therefore significantly reduced for these records.
I request that any reasonably segregable portion of any responsive record be provided even if other portions are withheld. Please identify the specific exemption claimed for any withheld material.
Fee Waiver Request
I request a full waiver of search, duplication, and review fees pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii). I am a journalist and author researching a book on the 1997 North Hollywood shootout for public interest publication. Disclosure of these records is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of ATF's role in the investigation of the weapons used in one of the most significant armed confrontations in American law enforcement history. The requested records are not sought for commercial use.
If a fee waiver is not granted, I request to be notified before any fees exceeding $25.00 are incurred, and I agree to pay reasonable fees up to that amount without prior notification.
Format
I request records in electronic format where available. If records exist only in paper form, I request copies.
